title: stipa orientalis var. ladakhorum
intro: Stipa orientalis var. ladakhorum, also known as Ladakh feather grass, is a perennial grass native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Tibet. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ladakh feather grass include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal combinations to create medicinal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves.
